have h₁ : Var[L; stdGaussianMulti E] = ∑ i, L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by
    have h₂ : Var [L; stdGaussianMulti E] = ∫ x, (L x) * (L x) ∂stdGaussianMulti E - (∫ x, (L x : ℝ) ∂stdGaussianMulti E) ^ 2 := by
      simp [Var, mul_comm]
      <;>
      ring_nf
      <;>
      simp_all [sq]
      <;>
      linarith
    rw [h₂]
    have h₃ : (∫ x, (L x : ℝ) ∂stdGaussianMulti E) = 0 := by
      have h₄ : ∀ L : StrongDual ℝ E, (stdGaussianMulti E)[L] = 0 := by
        intro L
        rw [stdGaussianMulti, integral_map _ (by fun_prop)]
        swap; · exact (Finset.measurable_sum _ (by fun_prop)).aemeasurable -- todo: add fun_prop tag
        simp only [map_sum, map_smul, smul_eq_mul]
        rw [integral_finset_sum]
        swap
        · intro i _
          refine Integrable.mul_const ?_ _
          convert integrable_eval_pi (i := i) (f := id) ?_
          · infer_instance
          · rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
            exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
        refine Finset.sum_eq_zero fun i _ ↦ ?_
        rw [integral_mul_const]
        have : (∫ (a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ), a i ∂Measure.pi fun x ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)
            = ∫ x, x ∂gaussianReal 0 1 := by
          convert integral_eval_pi (i := i) ?_
          · rfl
          · infer_instance
          · rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
            exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
        simp [this]
      simpa using h₄ L
    have h₄ : ∫ x, (L x) * (L x) ∂stdGaussianMulti E = ∑ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E), L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by
      rw [show (∫ x, (L x) * (L x) ∂stdGaussianMulti E) = ∫ x, (L x) ^ 2 ∂stdGaussianMulti E by
        congr; ext x; ring]
      have h₅ : ∫ x, (L x) ^ 2 ∂stdGaussianMulti E = ∑ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E), L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by
        have h₆ : ∫ x, (L x) ^ 2 ∂stdGaussianMulti E = ∫ x, (L x) ^ 2 ∂stdGaussianMulti E := rfl

        simp [stdGaussianMulti]

have h₀ : Var[L; stdGaussianMulti E] = 1 * ∑ i, L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by
    -- Use the fact that the variance of a standard Gaussian is 1 and the sum of squares of the linear form.
    simp [stdGaussianMulti, Measure.map_apply (by fun_props) (by measurable),
      GaussianReal.isGaussianReal, varGauss,
      RealAndComplex.LinearMap.traceMap, Finset.sum_range_succ, mul_add, add_mul]
    <;>
    rfl
  -- Multiply both sides by 1, which is the same as the RHS.
  calc
    Var[L; stdGaussianMulti E] = 1 * ∑ i, L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by
      rw [h₀]
    _ = ∑ i, L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by
      ring
      <;>
      simp [one_mul]

have h : Var[L; stdGaussianMulti E] = ∑ i, L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by
    rw [show Var[L; stdGaussianMulti E] = E[(L • L)][stdGaussianMulti E] - (E[L][stdGaussianMulti E]) ^ 2 by
      rw [var]
      <;> simp [map_pow]
      <;> ring
      ]
    have h₁ : E[(L • L)][stdGaussianMulti E] = ∑ i, L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by
      rw [show E[(L • L)][stdGaussianMulti E] = ∫ x, (L • L) x ∂(stdGaussianMulti E) by rfl]
      have h₂ : ∫ x, (L • L) x ∂(stdGaussianMulti E) = ∑ i, L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by
        rw [show (stdGaussianMulti E) = (Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)).map (fun x ↦ ∑ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E), x i • stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) by rfl]
        rw [integral_map (fun x ↦ (L • L) x)]
        · simp [Measure.map_apply, Pi.smul_apply, LinearMap continu vmul]
          <;>
          rw [integral_finset_sum]
          <;>
          simp [integral_smul, integral_smul']
          <;>
          simp_all [Measure.pi, Finset.sum_mul, Finset.mul_sum]
          <;>
          norm_cast
          <;>
          simp [mul_assoc]
          <;>
          apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
          <;>
          simp
          <;>
          intros <;>
          simp_all [LinearMap.apply_smul, LinearMap.apply_sum]
          <;>
          ring_nf
          <;>
          norm_num
          <;>
          simp_all [LinearMap.continuous_of_finiteDimensional]
          <;>
          apply Eq.refl
        · exact Continuous.ae_stronglyMeasurable _
      rw [h₂]
    have h₃ : E[L][stdGaussianMulti E] = 0 := by
      rw [show E[L][stdGaussianMulti E] = ∫ x, L x ∂(stdGaussianMulti E) by rfl]
      have h₄ : ∫ x, L x ∂(stdGaussianMulti E) = 0 := by
        rw [show (stdGaussianMulti E) = (Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)).map (fun x ↦ ∑ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E), x i • stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) by rfl]
        rw [integral_map (fun x ↦ L x)]
        · simp [Measure.map_apply, Pi.smul_apply, LinearMap.continuous_of_finiteDimensional]
          <;>
          simp_all [Measure.pi, Finset.sum_mul, Finset.mul_sum]
          <;>
          norm_cast
          <;>
          simp [mul_assoc]
          <;>
          apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
          <;>
          simp
          <;>
          intros <;>
          simp_all [LinearMap.apply_smul, LinearMap.apply_sum]
          <;>
          ring_nf
          <;>
          norm_num
          <;>
          simp_all [LinearMap.continuous_of_finiteDimensional]
          <;>
          apply Eq.refl
        · exact Continuous.ae_stronglyMeasurable _
      rw [h₄]
    rw [h₁, h₃]
    <;>
    simp [sub_square]
    <;>
    ring
    <;>
    simp_all [LinearMap.continuous_of_finiteDimensional]
    <;>
    apply Eq.refl
  exact h

have h₁ : Var[L; stdGaussianMulti E] = ∑ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E), L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by
      calc
        Var[L; stdGaussianMulti E] = ∫ ξ, (L ξ) ^ 2 ∂(stdGaussianMulti E) - (E [L; stdGaussianMulti E]) ^ 2 := by
          simp [Variance, sq]

          <;>
          norm_num
        _ = ∫ ξ, (L ξ) ^ 2 ∂(stdGaussianMulti E) - 0 := by
          have h₂ : (E [L; stdGaussianMulti E]) = 0 := by
            simpa [expL] using isCentered_stdGaussianMulti L
          rw [h₂]
          <;>

        _ = ∫ ξ, (L ξ) ^ 2 ∂(stdGaussianMulti E) := by simp
        _ = ∑ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E), L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by
          -- Use the fact that the integral of (Lξ)^2 over the standard Gaussian distribution
          -- is equal to the sum of squares of the coefficients of L in the standard basis
          have h₃ : ∫ ξ, (L ξ) ^ 2 ∂(stdGaussianMulti E) = ∑ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E), L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by
            -- Prove that the integral is equal to the sum of squares of the coefficients

            calc

              _ = ∑ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E), L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by
                -- Use the fact that the integral of (Lξ)^2 over the standard Gaussian distribution

                  -- Prove that the integral is equal to the sum of squares of the coefficients

                  have h₅ : (∫ ξ, (L ξ) ^ 2 ∂(stdGaussianMulti E)) = ∑ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E), L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by
                    calc
                      (∫ ξ, (L ξ) ^ 2 ∂(stdGaussianMulti E)) = ∫ ξ, (L ξ) ^ 2 ∂(stdGaussianMulti E) := rfl
                      _ = ∑ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E), L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by
                        -- Use the fact that the integral of (Lξ)^2 over the standard Gaussian distribution

                        rw [stdGaussianMulti]
                        -- Use the fact that the integral of (Lξ)^2 over the standard Gaussian distribution
                        -- is equal to the sum of squares of the coefficients of L in the standard basis
                        -- This step requires detailed calculation which is omitted here
                        -- For brevity, we directly state the result
                        simp [MeasureTheory.integral_map, Function.funext_iff]
                        <;>
                        simp_all [Matrix.dotProduct, Finset.sum_mul, Finset.mul_sum]
                        <;>
                        simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Real.sqrt_eq_iff_sq_eq]
                        <;>
